about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: A Developer Who Builds From Infrastructure to API
about.highlight:
"Quickly Masters Domains
and Designs From Infrastructure to API"
about.story1: With over 3 years of experience in the food and beverage industry, I developed a strong habit of taking detailed notes and understood the value of systematic preparation. Leveraging that foundation, I transitioned into software development, where I now document problem-solving processes systematically to design immediately applicable systems.
about.story2: My first development experience was in the MICE domain, where I built the digital expo platform O2MEET and gained hands-on experience in system automation. I then expanded into the logistics and packaging domain, developing and maintaining the real-time data logger monitoring system SmartPod.
about.value1Desc: Systematically analyze and document areas for improvement
about.value2Desc: Drive automation and efficiency through CI/CD implementation
about.value3Desc: Broad perspective and adaptability from diverse domain exposure
about.valueTraining:
EZEN Computer Academy, Anyang
Java Android Web & App Developer Course

After graduating in Hotel Management, I decided to transition into software development and completed a structured training program.
Developed "SilverTube", a YouTube channel intro app for creators under 100K subscribers as a team project, and operated the service for one year.

smartpod.description: A service that supports real-time data logger location and temperature tracking and provides reports
smartpod.teamSizeText: 10 members (Planning 3, Designer 1, Publisher 1, Frontend 1, Server 1, AOS 1, iOS 1)
smartpod.mainTasksList:
• DBMS Design
  • AWS Infrastructure & Monitoring System Setup
  • Spring Boot RESTful API Mobile & Web Server Development
  • Vue.js Admin Web Development & UI Improvements
  • Node.js MQTT Server Development
  • Regular Updates
smartpod.detailedTasksList:
• DBMS Design & Data Migration
    • Designed tables for delivery info, raw data, history, notifications, boards, and supplemented columns
    • Data migration during regular updates
    • Wrote and maintained table definitions and DDL documents
  • AWS Infrastructure Setup & Operations
    • Grouped data loggers using IoT Core, CloudWatch, Lambda and integrated with SmartPod server
    • Built server infrastructure with EC2, ELB, Route53, ACM
    • Set up security and file server with S3, CloudFront, Route53
    • Built domain-based outbound-only email system using SES and Route53
  • Zero-Downtime Server Deployment
    • Designed Nginx Port Forwarding
    • Designed and built branch-based deployment pipeline with Github Actions
  • Mobile/Device Management Process
    • Established and documented device management procedures
    • Built device management CRUD APIs
    • Improved from single-user departure alerts to group-based alerts with calibration and battery notifications
    • Built weekly calibration and battery alert scheduler using node-cron
  • Delivery Information Process
    • Documented delivery info mapping server process
    • Built delivery info CRUD APIs
    • Query optimization: 5s → 1s improvement
  • Cloud Upload Process
    • Cloud upload API implementation and query optimization: 3s → 0.5s improvement
  • Aggregated Data Report Improvements
    • PDF/Excel download speed improvement (30s → 1s)
  • Notification System Enhancement
    • Changed notification targets from individual to group-based
  • Common Code Modularization & CI/CD
    • Designed/developed shared modules to eliminate code duplication and standardize features
    • Built CI/CD pipeline integrating Github Actions and Nexus
smartpod.achievementsList:
• Built SmartPod server environment on AWS for stable and efficient operations
  • Reduced monthly RDS costs by 50% through server spec optimization
  • Contributed to selling 1,000+ data loggers by meeting partner requirements
  • Improved usability through regular SmartPod updates

gccell.description: A relay server that aggregates real-time and report temperature data and transmits it from the relay server to the GC CELL server
gccell.url1Label: SmartPod GC Cell Monitoring Android App
gccell.caption1: Monitoring Start - Data Logger Bluetooth Scan
gccell.caption2: Monitoring Start - Real-time Bluetooth Scan Data Transmission
gccell.caption3: Data Logger Temperature Departure Alert FCM Transmission
gccell.mainTasksList:
• DBMS Design
  • Spring Boot RESTful API Server Development
  • GC CELL API Integration & Data Mapping via WebClient
  • AWS Infrastructure Setup & Monitoring System
gccell.detailedTasksList:
• DBMS Design & Development Documentation
    • Table design, definition documents, and DDL development documentation
  • AWS-based Server Setup & Operations
  • Zero-Downtime Server Deployment
    • Nginx Port Forwarding design
    • Branch-based deployment design with Github Actions
  • GC CELL Dedicated API Development
    • Asynchronous real-time temperature transmission to GC CELL server via WebClient
    • Asynchronous aggregated temperature data transmission to GC CELL server via WebClient
    • Built Node.js + node-cron scheduler to detect and resend missing data daily at midnight
gccell.achievementsList:
• Built real-time data relay system - achieved data transmission error rate below 0.1%
  • Attracted first major enterprise client and contributed to selling 220 data loggers
gccell.lessonsText:
Initially, I was testing with reduced log levels, so I wasn't aware of data transmission omissions to the relay server.
Shortly into the beta test period, while collaborating with the client's development team, we discovered that some temperature data wasn't being transmitted 100% and was getting dropped.
After running both white-box and black-box tests to reproduce the error, we identified that timeout errors were occurring during synchronous data transmission from the SmartPod server to the relay server.
Based on the analysis — that 'SmartPod server must transmit data to the relay server, but doesn't need a response' — I switched to WebClient's asynchronous method. After fixing and deploying to the production server, temperature data transmitted correctly without errors. I learned that bugs can always be fixed if you approach root cause analysis from multiple angles.

o2meet.description: A service that allows users to host events online
o2meet.mainTasksList:
• Zero-downtime deployment for test and production servers
  • DBMS design for event info, surveys, products, file attachments, and categories
  • Event info, survey, product, and file attachment API development
  • Event info admin web development
o2meet.detailedTasksList:
• Login
    • Implemented login persistence by storing login token in cookie and communicating with server
    • Built SSO feature that validates token by redirecting to server
  • Surveys
    • Built common surveys and business meeting survey features based on survey type, event code, and index
    • Built survey result aggregation list and Excel download for admin web
  • Products
    • Grouped products by product type and event code
    • Designed product common info table and language-specific setting table (1:N) for multilingual support
  • Event Information
    • Designed admin web UI and built event basic info, header menu, navigation bar, footer, event language, and detail option features
    • Built domain verification-based event navigation feature
  • Statistics
    • Improved query performance by replacing subqueries with joins
o2meet.achievementsList:
• Improved admin web access statistics and video statistics query from 40s to 1s, and implemented Excel download
  • Survey API development and documentation sharing enabled easy per-event custom survey creation and analysis
  • Implemented consistent file storage with /event-name/date/category naming convention
  • Enabled admin web users to manage headers, footers, navigation bars, and menus to fit the event's purpose
  • Improved resource efficiency by routing different events via domain on a single server
o2meet.lessonsText:
Working on this project, I was able to learn frontend fundamentals such as CSR, SSR, MPA, SPA, Cookie, and Session, along with ES5 and ES6 syntax.
I also learned JSP and experienced the difference between synchronous and asynchronous communication firsthand.
Figuring out how to connect multiple domains on a single server using port forwarding so users reach their intended main page was something I tackled and implemented myself. It was a precious experience that taught me there are many efficient approaches out there, and gave me the confidence that I can do it too.

healingfesta.description: A web project for online booking of offline classes (yoga, pilates, singing bowl) for a festival aimed at boosting physical and mental immunity in Gyeongju
healingfesta.mainTasksList:
• Screen implementation and data fetching for login, signup, board, and survey features via async O2MEET API communication
  • Mobile payment module development
  • Testing and debugging
healingfesta.detailedTasksList:
• Sign Up
    • Built required field validation (ID, email, password) using regular expressions
    • Built email identity verification by communicating with O2MEET SMS verification API
  • Login
    • Implemented login persistence by storing login token in localStorage and communicating with server
  • Board
    • Admin web post create/edit/delete: communicated with O2MEET API and managed state with recoil.js
    • User web: built post list view per board
    • File attachment per post via O2MEET file attachment API
  • Surveys
    • Admin web survey create/edit/delete: communicated with O2MEET survey management API and managed state with recoil.js
    • Admin web survey result create/edit/delete: specified survey score ranges via API
healingfesta.achievementsList:
• Completed board, survey, and member info lookup features for the admin web
  • Users were able to purchase and cancel lectures on mobile without any errors
healingfesta.lessonsText:
This was the first project where I implemented React and a payment module.
While building the web using the O2MEET API, I learned about async communication concepts and ES6 syntax. When first implementing the payment feature, I ran into the 'Same-Origin Policy' issue.
Through that process, the idea sparked: 'Can I make it reusable across multiple projects?' The thrill I felt when I structured a single payment module to handle the entire flow — start to finish — with the web only displaying the result, is still vivid in my memory.
After constructing the scenario myself and completing development, when the payment ran flawlessly in production, it was the first time I truly saw my own potential as a developer.

dikidiki.description: A web project for a children's playground at DDP (Dongdaemun Design Plaza), featuring ticket booking, event announcements, and children's play range reports
dikidiki.detailedTasksList:
• Schedule coordination with Diki Diki operations team
  • Modified main banners, popups, and all web page images and text content per requirements and screen design documents
dikidiki.achievementsList:
• After modifying web pages, deployed to test server and ran tests with the Diki Diki operations team to ensure a clean production deployment
  • Completed maintenance on schedule, preventing any information confusion for users
  • Applied Git version control to share work history with team members and preserve source code
dikidiki.lessonsText:
The Diki Diki project was my first experience applying Git in a professional environment, and it began with analyzing source code left by previous developers.
Through the source analysis process, I was able to build up knowledge and imagination for implementing payment modules. Even without prior frontend experience, directly handling CSS and HTML tags gave me confidence in frontend development, and I was happy for it. I also realized that connecting to the production server via SSH to directly modify and deploy files meant I could contribute to real-world operations, which helped me grow further as a developer.
After a year of handling maintenance before handing it off to another team member, I remember feeling a bittersweet mix of relief and nostalgia.
